words.def: the time when somebody in a group of people should or is allowed to do something
words.example: When it's your turn, take another card.

words.def: television
words.example: We spent the evening watching TV.
There are only twelve of these rare animals left.
There are only two cookies left.

words.def: an object with a round folding frame of long, straight pieces of metal covered with material, that you use to protect yourself from the rain or from hot sun
the brother of your mother or father; the husband of your aunt or uncle
below something
words.example: He pulled up the covers and crawled under.

words.def: used when the speaker or writer and another or others are the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
words.example: She gave us a picture as a wedding present.

words.def: in the way that is usual or normal; most often
words.example: How long does the journey usually take?

words.def: a period of time spent travelling or resting away from home
words.example: on vacation, They're on vacation in Hawaii right now.

words.def: a plant or part of a plant that is eaten as food. Potatoes, beans and onions are all vegetables.
words.example: The children don't eat enough fresh vegetables.

words.def: a system of recording moving pictures and sound, either using a digital method of storing data or (in the past) using videotape
words.example: A wedding is the perfect subject for video.

words.def: an occasion or a period of time when somebody goes to see a place or person and spends time there
a man whose job is to serve customers at their tables in a restaurant, etc.
words.example: I'll ask the waiter for the bill.

words.def: to stop sleeping; to make somebody stop sleeping
words.example: I always wake early in the summer.

words.def: a journey on foot, usually for pleasure or exercise
a long, solid structure that rises straight up from the ground, made of stone, brick or concrete, that surrounds, divides or protects an area of land
a type of small clock that you wear on your wrist, or (in the past) carried in your pocket
words.example: She kept looking anxiously at her watch.

words.def: I and another person or other people; I and you
to have something on your body as a piece of clothing, a decoration, etc.
words.example: He was wearing a new suit.

words.def: the condition of the atmosphere at a particular place and time, such as the temperature, and if there is wind, rain, sun, etc.
the day of the week after Tuesday and before Thursday
a period of seven days, either from Monday to Sunday or from Sunday to Saturday
Saturday and Sunday
used in questions to ask for particular information about somebody/something
at what time; on what occasion
words.example: When did you last see him?

words.def: in or to what place or situation
used in questions to ask somebody to be exact about one or more people or things from a limited number
words.example: Which is better exercise—swimming or tennis?

words.def: having the colour of fresh snow or of milk
used in questions to ask about the name, identity or function of one or more people
used in questions to ask the reason for or purpose of something
the woman that somebody is married to; a married woman
words.example: I met my wife at university.

words.def: an opening in the wall or roof of a building, car, etc., usually covered with glass, that allows light and air to come in and people to see out; the glass in a window
words.example: out of the window, She looked out of the window.

words.def: the coldest season of the year, between autumn and spring
in the company or presence of somebody/something
an adult female human
very good, pleasant or a lot of fun
words.example: This is a wonderful opportunity to invest in new markets.

words.def: a single unit of language that means something and can be spoken or written
words.example: Do not write more than 200 words.

words.def: the job that a person does especially in order to earn money
words.example: He started work as a security guard.

words.def: a person who works, especially one who does a particular kind of work
the earth, with all its countries, peoples and natural features
used in polite offers or invitations
to make letters or numbers on a surface, especially using a pen or a pencil
words.example: In some countries children don't start learning to read and write until they are six.

words.def: a person whose job is writing books, stories, articles, etc.
the period from 1 January to 31 December, that is 365 or 366 days, divided into 12 months
having the colour of lemons or butter
used to answer a question and say that something is correct or true
on the day before today
words.example: A company spokeswoman said yesterday that no final decision had been made yet.

words.def: used as the subject or object of a verb or after a preposition to refer to the person or people being spoken or written to
words.example: You said you knew the way.

words.def: of or belonging to the person or people being spoken or written to
used when the person or people being spoken to both cause and are affected by an action
